scenes.text: Far downstream, in a quiet village, an old widow named Mbok Rondo lived alone. She spent her days fishing by the river, grateful for whatever little the water gave her.
One afternoon, something unusual caught in her net — a small snail, glowing faintly with golden light. She had never seen anything like it.
"You are beautiful, little one," she whispered, gently placing the snail in an earthen jar by the window. "Stay with me. I could use the company."
